prompt:
{content}
{known_entities_section}
Analyze this document and extract all organizational entities mentioned. You should identify:
PEOPLE: - Any person mentioned by name (full or partial) - References like "the CTO", "our lawyer", "the new hire" - Include role/title context if available
PROJECTS: - Named initiatives, projects, or programs - Examples: "Q4 migration", "API v2", "Chicago office move", "customer portal redesign" - Include any project with a specific name or clear identifier
TEAMS: - Departments, groups, or organizational units - Examples: "legal team", "engineering", "sales ops", "the board", "leadership team" - Include both formal and informal team references
YOU MUST RETURN YOUR RESPONSE IN THIS EXACT YAML FORMAT, INCLUDING THE YAML BLOCK MARKERS: ```yaml people: - id: "person-sarah-chen" name: "Sarah Chen" role: "Marketing Lead" - id: "person-mark" name: "Mark (Sales)" role: "Sales Representative" projects: - id: "project-q4-migration" name: "Q4 Migration" type: "infrastructure" - id: "project-api-v2" name: "API v2" type: "development" teams: - id: "team-engineering" name: "Engineering" type: "department" - id: "team-sales-ops" name: "Sales Ops" type: "group" ```
IMPORTANT RULES: 1. Always start with ```yaml on a new line 2. Always end with ``` on a new line 3. Always include all three root keys: people, projects, teams (even if empty) 4. Always use the correct prefix for IDs: - People: "person-" prefix - Projects: "project-" prefix - Teams: "team-" prefix 5. Use lowercase hyphenated format for all IDs 6. Include every entity mentioned - when in doubt, include it 7. For people, remove role information from the ID but keep it in the name field 8. For projects/teams, remove generic suffixes like "project", "team", "department" from the ID
